description: Co-occurrence of maximum decentralisation score with corporate ownership—suggests definitional overlap between product marketing and control reality.
interpretation: Discuss legal wrappers, token distribution, and whether 'decentralised' refers to protocol vs operator.

pattern_name: Governance token type but team-controlled process
description: Tokens classified as governance assets while formal governance remains team-steered.
interpretation: Useful tension for token design vs participation narratives; not an error but a structural pattern.

pattern_name: Centralised decentralisation score with on-chain token governance
description: On-chain voting exists while overall decentralisation is labelled centralised—often upgradeability or off-chain execution.
interpretation: Interpret via timelocks, multisigs, and scope of on-chain votes.

pattern_name: DAO ownership without formal governance enum
description: Ownership attributed to a DAO while governance type is recorded as none.
interpretation: May reflect data lag, informal governance, or classification edge cases.

pattern_name: High market cap per active wallet (top decile among measured)
description: Valuation per user in the top decile relative to the strict sample—signals speculative depth or thin active-user bases.
interpretation: Compare to category norms; avoid single-name attribution at thesis level.

pattern_name: Protocol TVL multiples above token market cap
description: TVL materially exceeds floated valuation—common for wrappers, staking, or measurement timing effects.
interpretation: Discuss leverage, double-counting, and snapshot mismatch between DeFi metrics and equity-like mcap.

pattern_name: Unfunded rows above funded median market cap
description: Share of zero-raised DApps whose market cap exceeds the median of funded peers in the same strict universe.
interpretation: Highlights retail-driven or organic listings vs VC-funded cohorts.

pattern_name: Centralised label with very high user counts
description: Some of the most popular DApps by active wallets are labelled centralised — users flock to them regardless of governance model.
interpretation: Adoption is driven by product quality and network effects, not decentralisation claims. Challenges the assumption that users care about governance.

description: In the strict sample, 86.8% of DApps are NOT fully decentralised, despite being built on decentralised infrastructure.
interpretation: The blockchain layer is decentralised but the application layer remains centralised — raising the question of whether DApp decentralisation is achievable or even desired by builders.

description: Gaming attracts 12,670,611 users with low per-user value; DeFi processes $299.1B volume. The value-per-user gap exceeds 100x between these sectors.
interpretation: User counts alone are misleading as a success metric. A DApp with 1M gaming users may generate less economic activity than a DEX with 10K traders.

pattern_name: Multi-chain deployment correlates with higher valuation
description: 70.6% of the strict sample deploys multi-chain. Legacy analysis shows a 1.3x market cap premium for multi-chain DApps.
interpretation: Unclear whether multi-chain causes growth or is simply affordable only for already-successful projects. Survivorship bias may inflate the premium.
